title: HA Cluster State and Session Synchronization Review
description: Inspect PAN-OS high-availability cluster state, HA4 transport, sync health, and corrective cache or sync actions.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook reviews Palo Alto HA behavior with #show high-availability cluster state, #show high-availability cluster session-synchronization, and related HA4 and statistics commands.

  1. Confirm cluster posture: inspect peer state, configuration, and flap history.
  2. Validate transport: check HA4 and HA4-backup health plus synchronization counters.
  3. Apply controlled recovery: clear stats, clear cache, or request sync-from only when the evidence supports it.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Review HA state and transport
> show high-availability cluster all
> show high-availability cluster state
> show high-availability cluster flap-statistics
> show high-availability cluster ha4-status
> show high-availability cluster ha4-backup-status

# Step 2: Review synchronization and counters
> show high-availability cluster session-synchronization
> show high-availability cluster statistics

# Step 3: Apply corrective maintenance if needed
> clear high-availability cluster statistics
> request high-availability cluster clear-cache
> request high-availability cluster sync-from
prerequisites: CLI access to an HA-enabled Palo Alto cluster
Authority to run HA maintenance commands if remediation is needed
detectionNotes: HA maintenance actions should be tightly audited
Unexpected cache clears or sync requests can disrupt an otherwise healthy cluster

steps.name: Query Cluster State
steps.tooltip: This establishes the baseline HA posture before transport or sync troubleshooting.
steps.description: The operator queries overall cluster configuration and current HA state from the active peer perspective.
steps.cli:
> show high-availability cluster all
> show high-availability cluster state

steps.name: Inspect Flap History and HA4 Health
steps.tooltip: Transport instability often explains inconsistent failover behavior or missed state replication.
steps.description: Flap statistics and HA4 / HA4-backup status reveal whether peer communication is unstable or impaired.
steps.cli:
> show high-availability cluster flap-statistics
> show high-availability cluster ha4-status
> show high-availability cluster ha4-backup-status

steps.name: Replicate Session State Between Peers
steps.tooltip: This step represents the state-replication path that keeps failover continuity intact.
steps.description: The active peer pushes synchronized session information through the HA session-synchronization subsystem.

steps.name: Update Passive Peer Session View
steps.tooltip: Successful synchronization is the core requirement for seamless HA session continuity.
steps.description: Replicated session state is made available to the passive peer so it can assume traffic handling if failover occurs.

steps.name: Expose Cluster Statistics
steps.tooltip: Counters help distinguish peer state issues from transport-side issues.
steps.description: HA transport behavior and cluster counters expose dropped packets and other sync-related anomalies.

steps.name: Apply Corrective HA Maintenance
steps.tooltip: Maintenance actions should be used only after state and transport evidence are understood.
steps.description: The operator clears statistics or session cache and requests synchronization if the evidence supports a maintenance intervention.
steps.cli:
> clear high-availability cluster statistics
> request high-availability cluster clear-cache
> request high-availability cluster sync-from

steps.name: Compile HA Health Report
steps.tooltip: This closes the workflow with a clear operational conclusion about cluster stability.
steps.description: The combined cluster, transport, and synchronization output is consolidated into an HA health report for the operator.
